column15: Description
column16: Acceptance Criteria

column15:
The Vanguard client web application: a responsive single-page portal where retail investors view accounts, holdings, and transactions; research the fund lineup; manage beneficiaries, automatic investing, RMDs, and tax documents; open new accounts; and read education content - all without calling in. This is the surface every other capability plugs into (the chat assistant, autopilot task fulfillment, and fund data all render here).

Value: deflects routine call volume, lets investors self-serve 24/7, and raises satisfaction by making the most common account actions doable in a few clicks.

=== ARCHITECTURE & STACK ===
This is the customer SPA (one of two React apps). Stack: React 18 + Vite + TypeScript, Zustand for
state, React Router v6 (BrowserRouter, basename = import.meta.env.BASE_URL). Built as a static bundle
deployed to the web root (GitHub Pages or S3+CloudFront).
State: clientStore (Zustand) loads the whole client profile once via fetchAll() and exposes selectors;
chatStore owns chat UI state. Components read stores and call the API; they hold no business logic.
Data path: ALL reads/writes go browser -> API Gateway (HTTP API) -> client-data Lambda -> DynamoDB
Clients table (PK clientId). Never call DynamoDB from the browser.
Shell: an AccessGate wraps the router; a ScrollToTop listener resets scroll on route change; theme
tokens live in one module; the build SHA is stamped on window for prod verification.
Demo data: seed/reset per environment via GET /reset-client-data?key=...
column16:
Definition of done:
- Every route below is reachable from the global navigation and renders live account data.
- The app is gated behind an access check and is usable on desktop and mobile widths.
- No page shows placeholder/mock data where a real DynamoDB-backed value exists.

column15:
The application frame that hosts every page: global top navigation, client-side routing, the access gate that protects the demo, scroll-to-top on navigation, web-font loading, and the build-SHA stamp used to confirm exactly what is deployed.

Value: a consistent, fast, secure shell is the precondition for every other portal capability.

=== TECHNICAL SCOPE ===
Components: AccessGate, a top-nav component, a ScrollToTop route listener, a theme-tokens module.
AccessGate compares the entered code to a build-time env var (VITE_DEMO_CODE); on success it writes a
localStorage flag and renders children; the flag short-circuits the gate on return visits.
Build stamp: set window.__BUILD__ and console.log("build " + sha); inject the commit SHA via a Vite
define at build time (CI passes it from the workflow context).
column16:
Definition of done:
- All routes are registered and 404s are handled gracefully.
- The access gate blocks unauthenticated access and remembers a valid entry per browser.
- window.__BUILD__ reflects the deployed commit SHA.

column8: Gate portal access behind an access code
column15:
As a platform owner, I want the client portal to require a valid access code before any page renders, so that only authorized people can reach the demo environment and we can detect first-time sign-ins.

On a correct code the gate persists a flag in localStorage so the prompt is not shown again on that browser; on a fresh/cleared browser a correct code fires a one-time client-log signin event.

=== TECHNICAL DESIGN ===
Implement AccessGate as a wrapper around <Routes>. Read VITE_DEMO_CODE at build time. State: an
"unlocked" boolean seeded from localStorage (e.g. key portal_access). On submit compare; on match set
localStorage + state; on miss show an inline error and keep the gate. Store only an opaque flag, never
the code itself.
Signin beacon: only when the flag was NOT already set (first unlock on this browser) AND only in PROD
(guard on import.meta.env), fire-and-forget POST /client-log {context:"access-code-entered"} using
fetch keepalive so it never blocks render.
column16:
Scenario: First visit requires the code
  Given I have never entered the access code on this browser
  When I open any portal URL
  Then I am shown the access gate and no portal content is rendered

Scenario: Correct code unlocks and is remembered
  Given I am on the access gate
  When I enter the correct access code
  Then the portal renders and I am not prompted again on this browser

Scenario: Incorrect code is rejected
  Given I am on the access gate
  When I enter an incorrect code
  Then I remain on the gate with an error and no content is exposed

column8: Implement AccessGate component with localStorage flag
column15: Wrap the router in an access gate; persist an access flag in localStorage on success so the prompt is shown once per browser.

column8: Fire one-time access-code-entered client-log event on fresh unlock
column15: PROD-only fire-and-forget POST /client-log {context:"access-code-entered"} when the flag was not already set.

column8: Provide global top navigation across the portal
column15:
As an investor, I want a persistent top navigation bar with links to Home, Portfolio, Research, Account, and the help/resources areas, so that I can move between portal areas from anywhere without using the browser back button.

=== TECHNICAL DESIGN ===
One top-nav component rendered above <Routes>; use NavLink for active styling. Drive links from a small
declarative config array (Home, Portfolio, Research, Account, Resources/Help). Collapse to a menu under
a mobile breakpoint.
column16:
Scenario: Navigation is always available
  Given I am on any portal page
  Then the top navigation is visible and links to the major areas
  And the current section is visually indicated

column8: Reset scroll position and load fonts/branding on navigation
column15:
As an investor, I want each page to open scrolled to the top with consistent Vanguard typography, so that navigating feels like a fresh page rather than a mid-scroll jump.

=== TECHNICAL DESIGN ===
ScrollToTop: a component using useLocation + useEffect to window.scrollTo(0,0) on pathname change. Load
web fonts via a <link> injected in an effect (or in index.html). Keep theme tokens centralized.
column16:
Scenario: New route starts at the top
  Given I am scrolled down on one page
  When I navigate to a different route
  Then the new page is scrolled to the top

column8: Stamp the deployed build SHA into the app
column15:
As an engineer, I want the running app to log its build commit and expose it on window.__BUILD__, so that I can confirm exactly which commit is live in prod without guessing.

=== TECHNICAL DESIGN ===
Inject the git SHA via a Vite define (e.g. __BUILD_SHA__) at build time; at startup set window.__BUILD__
and console.log it. CI provides the SHA from the workflow context (e.g. github.sha).
column16:
Scenario: Build SHA is observable
  Given the app has loaded
  When I open the browser console
  Then it logs "build <sha>" and window.__BUILD__ equals that SHA

column15:
The account hub and per-account detail pages: every account the investor holds (Roth IRA, Traditional IRA, SEP-IRA, Taxable), each balance and day change, and the holdings within each account. Detail pages link out to the self-service actions (beneficiaries, auto-invest, RMD, etc.).

Value: a single, trustworthy view of "what do I have and what is it worth" is the most-visited reason investors log in.

=== TECHNICAL SCOPE ===
Pages: Account hub (/account) and Account detail (/account/detail/:accountId). Data from clientStore
(accounts[], holdings[], totalBalance). Reuse a shared AccountCard + HoldingsTable. Detail filters
holdings + recent transactions by accountId and shows type-specific action links.
Shapes: account {type,balance,id,change}; holding {name,ticker,accountId,shares,price,change,value,drip?}.
column16:
Definition of done:
- The account hub lists every account with type, balance, and day change from live data.
- Each account detail page shows that account's holdings and recent transactions.
- Deep links to beneficiaries/auto-invest/RMD/tax-docs are present where applicable.

column8: View all accounts on an account hub
column15:
As an investor, I want a hub page listing every account I hold with its type, balance, and day change, so that I get a complete picture of my relationship in one place.

=== TECHNICAL DESIGN ===
Map clientStore.accounts to AccountCards; verify the displayed total equals the sum of balances;
right-align currency per the table rules; link each card to /account/detail/:id.
column16:
Scenario: Accounts are listed from live data
  Given I am a signed-in investor with multiple accounts
  When I open the Account page
  Then I see each account with its type, current balance, and day change
  And the totals reconcile with my portfolio total balance

column8: Drill into a single account's detail
column15:
As an investor, I want an account detail page showing that account's holdings and recent transactions, so that I can inspect a specific account without sifting through everything at once.

=== TECHNICAL DESIGN ===
Read :accountId from route params; filter holdings by accountId; show recent activity via
useRecentTransactions(accountId); render type-specific links (IRA/SEP -> beneficiaries, RMD; etc.).
column16:
Scenario: Account detail shows holdings and recent activity
  Given I am on the Account hub
  When I open a specific account
  Then I see its holdings (fund, shares, price, value) and its most recent transactions
  And links to the relevant self-service actions for that account type

column15:
A consolidated portfolio view: total balance across accounts, holdings, and a recent-transactions snapshot, plus the primary entry point to open a new account. Where the account hub is "my accounts", the portfolio is "my money in aggregate".

Value: gives investors the at-a-glance net position and a clear next action (invest more / open an account).

=== TECHNICAL SCOPE ===
Page /portfolio. Aggregate totalBalance + holdings across accounts from clientStore; a recent-
transactions snapshot (top N via useRecentTransactions) links to /transactions; an "Open an account"
button in the header navigates to /open-account. The chat FAB is global (rendered outside <Routes>).
column16:
Definition of done:
- Total balance and holdings aggregate correctly across all accounts.
- A recent-transactions snapshot links to the full Transaction History page.
- An "Open an account" action is present in the page header.

column8: See an aggregate portfolio with holdings and recent activity
column15:
As an investor, I want a portfolio dashboard with my total balance, holdings, and a snapshot of recent transactions, so that I understand my overall position at a glance.

=== TECHNICAL DESIGN ===
Aggregate holdings across accounts, sort by value; reuse HoldingsTable. Snapshot = top 5 recent
transactions with StatusCell. Right-align numeric/currency columns.
column16:
Scenario: Portfolio aggregates across accounts
  Given I hold positions in more than one account
  When I open the Portfolio page
  Then I see my combined total balance and a holdings list spanning all accounts
  And a recent-transactions section linking to the full Transaction History

column8: Start opening an account from the portfolio header
column15:
As an investor, I want an "Open an account" action in the portfolio header, so that I can begin opening a new account from the place I review my holdings.

=== TECHNICAL DESIGN ===
Header button -> navigate("/open-account"). Keep the global chat FAB mounted across the transition.
column16:
Scenario: Open-account entry point
  Given I am on the Portfolio page
  When I click "Open an account"
  Then I am taken to the account-opening wizard

column15:
The research surface: a screener over the full fund lineup, a per-fund profile page (objective, expense ratio, risk level, sector allocation, historical returns), and a buy entry point. All fund facts are read from the single fund catalog so pages never disagree with the chat assistant.

Value: lets investors discover and evaluate funds themselves, supporting confident, self-directed investing.

=== TECHNICAL SCOPE ===
Pages: /research (screener), /research/fund/:ticker (profile), /research/fund/:ticker/buy. All fund
facts via the useFunds() hook (catalog); live price/return via the market-data API. Screener filters in
component state over the funds array; profile shows catalog attributes + live quote; buy routes into the
purchase flow scoped to the ticker.
column16:
Definition of done:
- The screener lists every fund in the lineup with key attributes and supports filtering.
- Each fund profile renders catalog data plus live price/return from market data.
- A buy action routes into the purchase flow for that fund.

column8: Browse and filter the fund lineup
column15:
As a self-directed investor, I want a research screen that lists every fund with its asset class, expense ratio, and risk level and lets me filter, so that I can narrow the lineup to funds that fit my strategy.

=== TECHNICAL DESIGN ===
useFunds() for the list; filter/sort in component state (asset class, risk level, expense ratio). ~36
rows so no virtualization needed. Right-align numeric columns; link rows to the profile.
column16:
Scenario: Screener lists and filters funds
  Given I am on the Research page
  Then I see every fund in the lineup with asset class, expense ratio, and risk level
  When I apply a filter (e.g. asset class)
  Then only matching funds remain

column15:
As an investor, I want a fund profile page with the objective, expense ratio, risk level, sector allocation, historical returns, and live price, so that I can evaluate a fund in depth before investing.

=== TECHNICAL DESIGN ===
Read :ticker; find it in useFunds(); fetch the live quote from market-data; render sectorAllocation and
annualReturns; primary CTA -> /research/fund/:ticker/buy.
column16:
Scenario: Fund profile shows catalog + live data
  Given I open a fund profile from the screener
  Then I see the fund's catalog attributes and its current live price/return
  And a clear action to buy the fund

column8: Begin a purchase from a fund profile
column15:
As an investor, I want a buy page reachable from a fund profile, so that I can move from evaluating a fund to investing in it without leaving research.

=== TECHNICAL DESIGN ===
Buy page reads :ticker and prefills the fund; collects account/amount/funding; in the portal this is a
mock confirmation (the real brokerage path runs through an agent via execute-task).
column16:
Scenario: Buy entry point from profile
  Given I am on a fund profile
  When I choose to buy
  Then I land on the purchase flow pre-scoped to that fund

column15:
Dedicated functional pages for the account actions investors perform most: beneficiary designations, automatic investing schedules, Required Minimum Distribution settings, and tax-document retrieval. These pages read and write the same DynamoDB-backed data the autopilot uses, so the two channels agree.

Value: lets investors complete high-intent account maintenance on their own, the actions that otherwise generate the most calls.

=== TECHNICAL SCOPE ===
Pages: /account/beneficiaries, /account/auto-invest, /account/rmd, /account/tax-documents. Reads/writes
go through client-data Lambda actions; writes MUST mirror the execute-task real-write logic so the
portal and the autopilot never disagree. Beneficiary editor filters to IRA/SEP only and enforces 100%
per account; auto-invest supports create/edit/pause/resume; RMD shows eligibility + delivery/frequency/
withholding; tax docs list by year + form.
column16:
Definition of done:
- Beneficiaries, Auto-Invest, RMD, and Tax Documents each have a functional page bound to live data.
- Beneficiary edits enforce 100% allocation per account.
- These pages are reachable from account detail and from in-chat links.

column15:
As an IRA account holder, I want a beneficiaries page where I can view and edit primary and secondary beneficiaries per eligible account, so that I control who inherits each account without paperwork.

=== TECHNICAL DESIGN ===
Editor keyed by accountId; list only IRA/SEP accounts (never taxable). On save validate
sum(percentage) === 100 (block otherwise). Persist via a client-data update that atomically replaces
that account's beneficiaries while preserving the other accounts (identical semantics to execute-task
update-beneficiaries). Beneficiary shape {accountId,name,relationship,percentage,type}.
column16:
Scenario: Allocation must total 100%
  Given I am editing beneficiaries on an eligible account
  When I save a set of beneficiaries whose percentages do not total 100%
  Then the save is blocked with a clear validation message

Scenario: Taxable accounts are excluded
  Given I hold a taxable brokerage account
  Then it does not appear as a beneficiary-eligible account

column8: IRA/SEP account filter + per-account beneficiary editor UI
column15: Exclude taxable accounts; edit primary/secondary entries with percentages.
Block save unless the per-account percentages total exactly 100.
Replace only the target account beneficiaries; preserve all other accounts (match execute-task semantics).

As an investor, I want an auto-invest page to view, create, edit, pause, and resume recurring investments, so that I can keep dollar-cost-averaging on autopilot and adjust it when life changes.

=== TECHNICAL DESIGN ===
List autoInvest[] {id,accountId,accountType,fund,ticker,amount,frequency,dayOfMonth,nextDate,active}.
Actions create/update/pause-resume via client-data; recompute nextDate on change; reflect active state
in the UI.
column16:
Scenario: View and modify schedules
  Given I have at least one auto-invest schedule
  When I open the Auto-Invest page
  Then I see each schedule (fund, amount, frequency, next date, active state)
  And I can pause, resume, or edit a schedule

column15:
As a retiree with a Traditional or SEP IRA, I want an RMD page showing my required minimum distribution status and letting me set delivery, frequency, and withholding, so that I stay compliant with mandatory withdrawals without calling in.

=== TECHNICAL DESIGN ===
rmd object {eligible,age,annualRmd,takenThisYear,remainingThisYear,nextDeadline,deliveryMethod,
frequency,taxWithholding}. If !eligible, render an explanatory state. Writable: deliveryMethod,
frequency, taxWithholding (0-99%).
column16:
Scenario: RMD page reflects eligibility
  Given I hold a Traditional IRA or SEP-IRA that is RMD-eligible
  When I open the RMD page
  Then I see my RMD status and can set delivery method, frequency, and tax withholding

Scenario: Not RMD-eligible
  Given I hold only Roth or taxable accounts
  Then the page explains I have no current RMD obligation

column15:
As an investor, I want a tax-documents page listing my available forms (1099-R, 1099-B, 1099-DIV, 5498) by year, so that I can download what I need at tax time without contacting support.

=== TECHNICAL DESIGN ===
Derive available forms by year (1099-R/1099-B/1099-DIV/5498) from account types + activity; request/
download triggers a mock document action returning a reference number.
column16:
Scenario: List available tax forms
  Given I have reportable activity in a prior tax year
  When I open the Tax Documents page
  Then I see the relevant forms for each available year with a way to request/download them

column15:
A realistic, multi-step account-opening flow: choose account type, enter personal and contact information, acknowledge FINRA/SEC disclosures, complete account-type-specific setup (IRA beneficiaries with 100% allocation, SEP business info, taxable joint owner/TOD), fund the account and place an initial investment, opt into free dollar-cost-averaging, then review, sign, and confirm. Steps are URL-driven so browser back/forward and refresh work, and the in-flow chat tracks the exact step.

Value: converts prospective and existing investors into funded new accounts entirely online.

=== TECHNICAL SCOPE ===
Single route /open-account; 8 steps with step + completion encoded in the URL (?step=N, ?done=1) via
useSearchParams so browser Back/Forward and refresh traverse the wizard. Form state stays in component
state (a query-param change does not unmount the page). Branch by accountType at the setup step. Publish
a fine-grained page-context key per step + branch via a pageContextStore so chat pills track the exact
screen; in-flow topics are ungated and do not link back to /open-account.
column16:
Definition of done:
- The wizard branches correctly by account type and enforces type-specific rules.
- Step and completion state are encoded in the URL (?step / ?done) and survive refresh.
- The flow ends in a confirmation timeline; in-flow chat pills track the current step.

column8: Open an account through a guided multi-step wizard
column15:
As a prospective or existing investor, I want a guided wizard that walks me from account type through funding, agreements, and e-signature, so that I can open and fund a new account online without paper forms or a phone call.

=== TECHNICAL DESIGN ===
Steps: type -> personal -> contact -> FINRA/SEC disclosures -> setup(branch) -> funding + initial
investment -> free DCA opt-in -> review/agreements/e-signature -> confirmation. goTo(step)/handleSubmit
push merged search params. Branch components: IRA (beneficiaries with 100% allocation check), SEP
(business/EIN), Taxable (joint owner + TOD). Validate per step; block IRA progress until beneficiaries
total 100%. On submit, mock-create the account and render a confirmation timeline (?done=1).
column16:
Scenario: Account-type branch is enforced
  Given I choose to open a SEP-IRA
  When I reach the setup step
  Then I am asked for business/EIN information specific to a SEP-IRA

Scenario: IRA beneficiary allocation is validated
  Given I am opening an IRA and adding beneficiaries
  When their allocations do not total 100%
  Then I cannot proceed until they do

Scenario: Completion confirmation
  Given I have signed and submitted the application
  Then I see a confirmation timeline describing what happens next

column8: Build the 8-step wizard scaffold and step branching
column15: Type -> personal -> contact -> disclosures -> type setup -> funding+initial invest -> DCA opt-in -> review/sign/confirm.

column8: Drive step/done state from URL query params
column15: Use ?step=N and ?done=1 so browser back/forward and refresh traverse the wizard; form state stays in component state.

column8: Publish per-step page context for in-flow chat pills
column15: Publish open-account/<step> and open-account/setup-{ira,sep,taxable} keys via pageContextStore so chat topics track the screen.

column8: Encode wizard step + done in URL query params (useSearchParams)
column15: Drive ?step=N and ?done=1 from the URL so Back/Forward + refresh work; keep form values in component state.

column8: Build the 8 step components + account-type branch components
column15: type, personal, contact, disclosures, setup(IRA/SEP/Taxable), funding+initial invest, DCA opt-in, review/sign/confirm.

column8: Per-step validation incl. IRA 100% beneficiary allocation gate
column15: Block advancing past IRA setup until primary beneficiary allocations total 100%.

column8: Publish per-step page-context keys for chat pills
column15: Publish open-account/<step> and setup-{ira,sep,taxable} via pageContextStore; ChatWidget prefers it over the path key.
On submit, create the account (mock) and render the terminal confirmation timeline.

The content estate that powers self-service learning and backs the chat assistant's links: ~10 resource pages (IRA contribution limits, Roth IRA, SEP-IRA, SEP vs Solo 401(k), tax-efficient investing, estate planning, self-employed retirement, tax deductions, rollovers, and an interactive retirement calculator); a library of guide/opinion articles and an investor podcast; and ~27 help pages (account access, transfers, beneficiaries, cost basis, DRIP, fees, fund performance, prospectus library, RMD guide, rollover guide, statements, trading, wire transfer, withdrawals, etc.).

Value: deflects informational contacts, builds investor confidence, and gives the bot authoritative pages to link to instead of free-typing answers.

=== TECHNICAL SCOPE ===
Mostly static content components: ~10 resource pages, a library (index + ArticlePage by category guide/
opinion + slug, plus a podcast page), and ~27 help pages. Pages that show fund data use useFunds(). The
retirement calculator is interactive. Help/resource routes are the bot link targets (SELF_SERVICE_PAGES)
- keep slugs stable and aligned with the KB link table.
column16:
Definition of done:
- All resource, library, and help routes render and are linkable from chat.
- The retirement calculator computes projections from user inputs.
- Pages that show fund data read from the shared fund catalog.

column15:
As an investor, I want a set of resource pages covering IRAs, rollovers, tax-efficient investing, estate planning, and self-employed retirement, so that I can learn the concepts behind my decisions without leaving the portal.

=== TECHNICAL DESIGN ===
Static content components with a consistent layout; cross-link to related actions/pages; ensure routes
match the bot link table so in-chat links resolve.
column16:
Scenario: Resource pages are reachable and self-contained
  Given I follow a resource link from navigation or chat
  Then the page explains the topic and links to related actions/pages

column8: Project retirement outcomes with a calculator
column15:
As an investor planning for retirement, I want an interactive retirement calculator where I can enter ages, balances, and contributions, so that I can see a projection and adjust assumptions.

=== TECHNICAL DESIGN ===
Controlled inputs. The NumberInput must NOT min-clamp on each keystroke (support select-all + retype);
digits-only with a maxDigits cap; ages unclamped by design. Compute a future-value-with-contributions
projection and re-render on input change.
column16:
Scenario: Calculator recomputes on input
  Given I am on the retirement calculator
  When I edit an input (e.g. monthly contribution)
  Then the projected outcome updates accordingly

Scenario: Inputs accept full edits
  Given a numeric input has a value
  When I select all and retype
  Then it is not min-clamped on each keystroke

column8: Browse the library and listen to the investor podcast
column15:
As an investor, I want a content library of guide and opinion articles plus a podcast page, so that I can engage with longer-form education in the format I prefer.

=== TECHNICAL DESIGN ===
Library index + ArticlePage (route params: category in {guide,opinion}, slug); a podcast page. Source
content from a data module or markdown.
column16:
Scenario: Library lists articles by category
  Given I open the Library
  Then I can browse guide and opinion articles and open any one to read it
  And I can reach the podcast page

column8: Find answers in the help center
column15:
As an investor, I want a help center with focused pages for common how-to topics (fees, beneficiaries, rollovers, statements, withdrawals, trading, and more), so that I can resolve specific questions on my own.

=== TECHNICAL DESIGN ===
~27 focused help-topic pages, each a stable route the bot can deep-link. Keep slugs aligned with the KB
link table so the bot never links to a 404.
column16:
Scenario: Help pages are focused and linkable
  Given the bot or navigation links me to a help page
  Then the page answers that specific topic and the link opens the correct page

column15:
End-to-end transaction history: a dedicated table (one item per transaction) that stores each account's activity back to inception, a deterministic decades-deep seed for the demo personas, and a per-transaction status reflecting the real mutual-fund order lifecycle (Scheduled, Pending, Settling, Completed, Canceled). The portal surfaces this on every transaction list and on a full Transaction History page with filtering, search, sorting, and pagination; each status has a click-to-explain popover. The same data (including status) is exposed to the chatbot and autopilot.

Value: answers "where is my trade / what happened to my money" accurately and self-serve, across both the portal and the assistant.

=== TECHNICAL SCOPE ===
Dedicated Transactions DynamoDB table, one item per transaction: PK clientId, SK "<ISOdate>#<seq>", GSI
account-index on acctKey="<clientId>#<accountId>"; newest-first = Query with ScanIndexForward:false.
Status engine: assignStatus(date vs a frozen DEMO_TODAY) -> Scheduled/Pending/Settling/Completed/
Canceled. Deterministic seed generator (idempotent, decades deep across personas) via the reset Lambda.
API: client-data actions get-recent-transactions / get-transactions-page (base64 cursor) /
append-transaction. Frontend: useRecentTransactions hook + StatusCell popover + the /transactions page.
Live trades (execute-task) PutItem a Pending row dated today.
column16:
Definition of done:
- Transactions are stored one-per-row with a per-account index and newest-first querying.
- The history page filters, searches, sorts, and paginates large histories.
- Status is computed deterministically and explained via an in-line popover.

column8: Browse full transaction history with filter/search/sort/paginate
column15:
As an investor, I want a Transaction History page that lets me filter, search, sort, and page through my entire history, so that I can find any past transaction quickly even across decades of activity.

=== TECHNICAL DESIGN ===
Page calls get-transactions-page with a base64 cursor; render a table with right-aligned amounts and
StatusCell; provide account filter, text search, column sort, and next/prev paging. Handle loading/
empty/error states.
column16:
Scenario: Paginate a deep history
  Given my account has years of transactions
  When I open Transaction History
  Then transactions load newest-first and I can page without loading everything at once

Scenario: Filter and search
  Given I am on Transaction History
  When I filter by account and search a description
  Then only matching rows are shown

column8: Create the transactions table with a per-account GSI
column15: One item per transaction: PK clientId, SK <ISOdate>#<seq>, GSI account-index on acctKey; newest-first via descending sort-key query.
get-recent-transactions and get-transactions-page (base64 cursor) in client-data Lambda.

column8: Build the Transaction History page UI
column15: Filter/search/sort/paginate over the paginated API via useRecentTransactions/history hooks.
Query newest-first (ScanIndexForward:false); return items + opaque cursor.

column8: Table UI with account filter, search, sort, paging
column15: Right-aligned amounts; StatusCell; loading/empty/error states.

As an investor, I want a status on every transaction with a click-to-explain popover, so that I understand whether an order is scheduled, pending, settling, completed, or canceled and what happens next.

=== TECHNICAL DESIGN ===
StatusCell: a dotted-underline label that opens a popover from a display-copy map (per status: meaning +
what happens next). Status value comes from assignStatus; expose it through the read APIs and the
get_transactions AI tool too.
column16:
Scenario: Status popover explains the lifecycle
  Given a transaction shows a status with a dotted underline
  When I click the status
  Then a popover explains what the status means and what happens next

column15: assignStatus by date vs frozen DEMO_TODAY in shared/transaction-status.ts.
Dotted-underline status with click popover; copy in data/transactionStatus.ts.

column8: Seed decades-deep transaction history for demo personas
column15:

column15:
A floating chat assistant available on every portal page. It opens an Amazon Connect chat session, answers questions instantly via an AI bot, links investors to self-service pages, and escalates to a live agent (or schedules a callback) the moment a request needs a human or an account change. The experience is engineered to feel human: realistic typing/reading delays, agent avatars and names, minimize/persist behavior, chat history, and the ability to resume a recent agent conversation.

Value: gives every investor an immediate, knowledgeable first responder, deflecting simple contacts and warming up the ones that escalate so agents start with full context.

=== ARCHITECTURE & STACK ===
Lives in the customer SPA. Transport: Amazon Connect ChatJS SDK. A useChatSession hook owns the Connect
session: start via POST /start-chat (creates the Connect contact, returns participant credentials), then
ChatJS connect/sendMessage/onMessage/onTyping. chatStore (Zustand) holds view state and is persisted to
sessionStorage (per tab) for resume.
Components: ChatWidget (global FAB, mounted OUTSIDE <Routes>), ChatPanel (header, minimize/close, view
switch chat/history/transcript), ChatBody (messages + typing + cards), ChatMessage, ChatInput,
TopicButtons/QuestionButtons, ContinueChatCard, ApprovalFormCard, ChatHistoryView, ChatMinimizedBar.
Pre-agent AI replies come from POST /autopilot-turn (full-auto scope). Control messages (agent name,
typing-stop sentinel, approval forms) are intercepted in onMessage and NOT rendered as chat bubbles.
GOTCHA: a customer ChatJS session ends via disconnectParticipant() - there is NO disconnect() (calling it
throws; a swallowing try/catch hid this for weeks). Treat ChatJS onEnded as "chat over" ONLY in
CONNECTED_TO_AGENT / WAITING_FOR_AGENT states - non-escalated contacts fire onEnded seconds after connect
while the bot keeps answering via the fallback path.
column16:
Definition of done:
- The widget is reachable from any page and reliably starts and ends Connect sessions.
- The bot answers, links, and escalates; live-agent UX is indistinguishable from a human.
- History, persistence, and resume all work across reloads and off-site navigation.

column15:
The chat surface and its full lifecycle: a floating action button that expands into a panel, session creation via the start-chat Lambda + Connect ChatJS, minimize-to-docked-bar with an unread badge, a close-confirmation dialog, true Connect disconnect on end, and per-tab persistence so a reload or a trip off-site and back resumes the conversation (with missed agent messages backfilled).

Value: a dependable, forgiving chat container that never loses a conversation is the foundation the rest of the assistant stands on.

=== TECHNICAL SCOPE ===
useChatSession owns start (POST /start-chat -> contactId + token -> ChatJS connect), send, receive
(onMessage), typing (onTyping/notifyTyping), end (disconnectParticipant). chatStore persists
{persist, minimized, unreadCount, chatEnded, messages} to sessionStorage. On load, if a live session
exists, reconnect and backfill missed agent messages via getTranscript, reconciling by message id. Tab
close (no persisted live session) = chat over. Close-confirm dialog offers Minimize (default) / End chat;
skip the dialog for never-engaged or already-ended chats.
column16:
Definition of done:
- Opening the widget starts (or resumes) exactly one Connect session.
- Minimize keeps the session live; close-after-engagement confirms; end truly disconnects.
- A reload or off-site round-trip restores the live conversation; tab close ends it.

column8: Close chat with an intentional confirmation
column15:
As an investor, I want a confirmation when I close an engaged chat offering minimize or truly end it, so that I do not accidentally end a live conversation, and ending it actually disconnects me.

=== TECHNICAL DESIGN ===
On close after engagement, show the confirm dialog. End chat -> endChat() -> disconnectParticipant() (NOT
disconnect()); do not swallow errors. Never-engaged or already-ended chats close silently (no dialog).

Scenario: End truly disconnects
  Given I choose End chat
  Then the Connect participant is genuinely disconnected

column15: Skip dialog for never-engaged or already-ended chats.
Customer ChatJS ends via disconnectParticipant() - there is no disconnect(); ensure errors are not swallowed.
Customer ChatJS has no disconnect(); call disconnectParticipant() and surface (do not swallow) errors.
Minimize (default) / End chat; skip the dialog for never-engaged or already-ended chats.

As an investor, I want my chat to persist per browser tab and resume on reload or when I navigate away and back, so that I do not lose an in-progress conversation due to a refresh or a quick detour.

via getTranscript; reconcile the message list by id (dedupe). A real tab close clears sessionStorage, so it
ends the chat.

Scenario: Tab close ends it

  When I close the browser tab
  Then the chat is treated as over

column15:
The pre-agent bot (autopilot full-auto scope) that handles conversations end-to-end: answering general questions about account types, IRAs, RMDs, rollovers, contributions, and taxes; directing investors to self-service pages with clickable in-chat links; and recognizing when a request needs escalation. It hard-routes trade requests and financial-advice questions to the proper channels and never claims it can make account changes itself.

Value: resolves the large tail of simple informational contacts without a human, at any hour.

=== TECHNICAL SCOPE ===
Pre-agent bot = autopilot-turn full-auto scope (FULL_AUTO_PROMPT), on Nova Micro or gpt-4o-mini. The prompt
embeds SELF_SERVICE_PAGES (the route table) so the bot LINKS instead of free-typing, plus the shared
FORBIDDEN_TOPICS block. In-chat links use a markdown-style link token the client renders as an <a> that
calls React Router navigate() (same-tab, in-app). The bot must not claim it can perform account changes;
trade/advice intents are detected and routed (advice -> callback scope).
column16:
Definition of done:
- The bot answers in-scope questions and links to the correct self-service page.
- Trade and advice intents are detected and routed, not answered.
- The bot never claims it can directly perform an account change.

when one fits the question.

column8: Be safely routed for trades and financial advice
column15:
As a compliance owner, I want the bot to refuse to give personalized investment advice or execute trades and instead route to the proper channel, so that we stay within regulatory boundaries while still helping the client.

=== TECHNICAL DESIGN ===
Detect advice/trade intent (ADVICE_RE keyword + LLM). Respond with the scripted FORBIDDEN_TOPICS line and
offer a callback with a licensed rep; never recommend a security or imply the bot can execute a trade.

column8: Escalation to Live Agent & Intent Summary
column15:
When a request needs a human or an account action, the bot offers escalation to a live agent or a callback. On escalation, a secondary LLM call generates an intent label and a suggested agent greeting so the agent's workspace is pre-populated with context before the first human word.

Value: shortens handle time and makes the handoff feel seamless - the agent already knows why the client is here.

=== TECHNICAL SCOPE ===

Micro and attaches them to the Connect contact attributes so the agent app shows context before the first
human word. Pass clientName explicitly to the model to avoid beneficiary/name confusion.

As an agent, I want an AI-generated intent label and suggested greeting waiting in my workspace when a chat arrives, so that I open the conversation already knowing the context and can greet the client by need.

column15:
The details that make a live (and autopilot-assisted) chat feel human: bidirectional typing indicators with a reading delay before a typing delay, avatars that switch from a "V" assistant mark to the connected agent's initials, and the agent's full name broadcast on connect so initials and attribution are correct.

Value: trust and warmth - clients engage more openly when the experience feels like a real person.

=== TECHNICAL SCOPE ===
Typing: customer notifyTyping on keystrokes -> agent maps onTyping to slot.customerTyping -> TypingDots
(30s expiry). Agent->customer typing via send-agent-message event:'typing' -> customer agentTyping (60s
expiry). Autopilot send (autopilotSend) runs two phases via autopilotDelay: a READING delay (ellipsis
hidden) = 2000ms + 10ms*(clientMsgLen-200), min 2000ms, based on the client's last message; then a TYPING
delay (ellipsis shown) = chars/15 seconds. Cancel sends a typing-stop sentinel to clear it promptly.
Avatars: an assistant mark for bot turns; once a human connects, agent bubbles + ellipsis show the agent's
initials (chatStore.agentName via an initialsFromName util). The agent broadcasts its FULL name on connect
as a control message because Connect DisplayName is only the first name.
column16:
Definition of done:
- Typing indicators appear/expire correctly in both directions.
- Autopilot replies show a reading delay then a typing delay before sending.
- Avatars and names reflect the connected agent for the rest of the chat.

column8: See when the agent is typing
column15:
As an investor, I want an animated typing indicator when the agent (or autopilot drafting on their behalf) is composing, so that I know a reply is coming and do not send duplicate messages.

=== TECHNICAL DESIGN ===
Customer ChatInput calls notifyTyping; agent useConnectStreams maps chatSession.onTyping to
slot.customerTyping and ChatColumn renders TypingDots (30s expiry). Reverse direction: customer ChatBody
shows agentTyping (60s expiry).
column16:
Scenario: Typing indicator lifecycle
  Given I am connected to an agent
  When the agent is composing
  Then I see an animated ellipsis that fades after ~60s of inactivity and reappears when they resume

column15:
As an investor, I want autopilot replies to arrive at a human pace - a reading pause before the typing indicator even appears, so that AI-assisted replies are indistinguishable from a person typing.

=== TECHNICAL DESIGN ===
Implement autopilotDelay with the reading-then-typing phases (formulas above). Keep the ellipsis hidden
during the reading delay; show it during the typing delay; then send via send-agent-message.
column16:
Scenario: Reading then typing delay
  Given autopilot has drafted the next agent message
  Then it first waits a reading delay (min ~2s, longer for longer client messages) with no ellipsis
  And only then shows the typing ellipsis during the typing delay before sending

column15: Ellipsis hidden; 2000ms + 10ms*(clientMsgLen-200), min 2000ms, based on the client last message.

column8: Implement autopilotDelay typing phase + cancel sentinel
column15: Show ellipsis; delay chars/15 s; on cancel send the typing-stop sentinel to clear it.

As an investor, I want the avatar and name to reflect the real agent once a human connects, so that I know I am talking to a named person, not a bot.

On agent connect, broadcast the agent's full name as a control message (intercepted client-side, not

column15:
If the client spoke with a live agent within the last 7 days, opening the chat shows a card (above the topic pills) with the date and a one-sentence recap and a "Continue this chat" button. Clicking it checks whether that specific agent is currently available and lets the client wait for them or take the first available agent; on accept, the prior transcript loads into the agent's workspace marked as a continued conversation. Purely additive - absent when there is no recent agent chat.

Value: continuity - clients pick up where they left off with the person who already knows their story.

=== TECHNICAL SCOPE ===
Continuation memory = lastAgentChat attribute on the Clients table, written by save-transcript at agent-
chat end {transcriptId,endedAt,summary,agentUsername,agentName}; cleared by reset-all-data (the permanent
transcript log is untouched). ContinueChatCard renders above the topic pills when lastAgentChat is within
7 days. On click: GET /agent-availability?username=... checks whether THAT agent is on queue/Available; the
client chooses wait-for-them vs first-available (auto first-available if unavailable). On agent accept the
prior transcript loads above a "Continued chat" divider (loadContinuationTranscript). NOTE: true per-agent
Connect routing is best-effort + a preferredAgentUsername metadata attribute today (documented follow-up).
column16:
Definition of done:
- The card appears only when a qualifying recent agent chat exists.
- Availability of the specific prior agent is checked; client can wait or take first-available.
- On accept, the prior transcript loads above a "continued chat" divider.

column15:
The core intelligence of the platform: a single Lambda that handles every AI-driven chat turn for both the pre-agent bot and the live-agent autopilot. For a live chat it identifies which of 19 account tasks the client needs, then drives a structured, reactive field-collection conversation until every required value is confirmed - at which point it hands the agent a ready-to-approve Proposed Action. It applies shared guardrails (financial advice, trades, fraud, inheritance) across every task and uses the right model for each job (Amazon Nova Micro for fast classification; OpenAI GPT for the conversational experts).

Value: this is what lets one agent supervise many conversations - the AI does the collecting, validating, and structuring; the agent does the judgment.

=== ARCHITECTURE & STACK ===
A single Node 20 Lambda (handler.ts, ~1900 lines) behind POST /autopilot-turn, scope-dispatched
(full-auto | get-intent | idle-check | callback | locate-evidence). Stateless and reactive: ONE invocation
per client message, no polling, no server-side state between turns (the transcript is passed in).
Models: Bedrock Nova Micro (invokeNovaMicro) for classification, full-auto, ACW, NBR, intent labels; OpenAI
gpt-4o for the get-intent task experts (gpt-4o-mini proved too weak for the experts' rules); gpt-4o-mini is
the default for bot/NBR/labels for cost. OPENAI_API_KEY resolves from SSM at deploy.
Shared modules: tasks.ts (TASKS catalog + matchTaskByIntent + filterFields), kb.ts, types.ts, client-tools
.ts (on-demand tool calling). Returns {reply, shouldExitAutopilot, proposedAction?, scope}.
INVARIANT (safety guard): never return shouldExitAutopilot=true without a concrete proposedAction - if that
happens, reset it to false. Wrap model calls in try/catch (holding message + safe exit on error).
column16:
Definition of done:
- The engine routes every scope correctly and never hands off without a complete Proposed Action.
- All 19 task experts collect their fields, enforce their rules, and exit cleanly.
- Shared guardrails apply identically across every task.

column15:
The dispatcher that runs every chat turn under one of several scopes (full-auto, get-intent, idle-check, callback, locate-evidence) in a single stateless, reactive Lambda. It selects the model per job (Nova Micro vs OpenAI GPT), applies a safety guard that prevents exiting autopilot without a concrete Proposed Action, and degrades gracefully on LLM error.

Value: a single well-structured engine is cheaper to operate, easier to reason about, and avoids the race conditions of a polling design.

=== TECHNICAL SCOPE ===
Top-level dispatch by scope. full-auto -> FULL_AUTO_PROMPT; get-intent -> two-phase task engine; idle-check
-> check-in; callback -> CALLBACK_PROMPT; locate-evidence -> post-hoc utility (early-returns before chat-
turn logic). Choose the model per job. Apply the safety guard before returning.
column16:
Definition of done:
- Each scope is dispatched correctly from a single handler.
- The safety guard resets a premature exit when no Proposed Action exists.
- An LLM failure yields a safe holding message rather than a broken turn.

column8: Run all chat turns through one scoped engine
column15:
As a platform owner, I want every AI chat turn to run through one scoped, stateless Lambda, so that behavior is consistent and the system stays simple to operate and reason about.

=== TECHNICAL DESIGN ===
Single handler; parse {scope, transcript, profile, ...}; branch by scope; keep it stateless so concurrent
turns never race.
column16:
Scenario: Scope dispatch
  Given an incoming chat turn with a scope
  When the engine runs
  Then it executes the matching behavior (full-auto, get-intent, idle-check, callback, or locate-evidence)

column8: Never hand off without a complete Proposed Action
column15:
As an agent, I want autopilot to never exit to me unless a concrete Proposed Action is ready, so that I never receive a handoff with nothing to act on.

=== TECHNICAL DESIGN ===
After the expert turn: if (shouldExitAutopilot && !proposedAction) shouldExitAutopilot = false. Add a unit
test for this exact case.
column16:
Scenario: Premature exit is blocked
  Given the expert signals completion but has produced no Proposed Action
  When the engine evaluates the exit
  Then it resets the exit to false and keeps collecting

column15:
As an investor, I want a calm holding message if the AI errors rather than a broken chat, so that a transient model failure never leaves me staring at an error.

=== TECHNICAL DESIGN ===
try/catch around the model call; on throw, reply with a brief holding line ("pulling some information...")
and set shouldExitAutopilot=true; log the error to CloudWatch.
column16:
Scenario: Safe fallback on error
  Given the LLM call throws during a turn
  Then the client receives a brief "pulling some information" style message and the turn ends safely

column15:
Phase 1 of the live-agent autopilot: determine which of the 19 tasks the client needs. A zero-LLM keyword matcher tries first (fastest, cheapest); an LLM fallback (Nova Micro) resolves the paraphrased cases ("give his wife access"). Financial-advice requests short-circuit to the callback scope before identification so "best stocks to buy" is not swallowed by a purchase task.

Value: fast, accurate task routing keeps cost down and gets the right expert on the conversation.

=== TECHNICAL SCOPE ===
matchTaskByIntent(intent, accountTypes) scores TASKS by keyword overlap (zero LLM). On miss,
identifyTaskWithLLM (Nova Micro) classifies. ADVICE_RE short-circuits "what should I buy" to the callback
scope BEFORE identification. Eligibility: tasks with eligibleAccountTypes only match clients holding those
types.
column16:
Definition of done:
- Keyword matching resolves common intents with no LLM call.
- The LLM fallback resolves paraphrased intents.
- Advice intents are routed to callback before task identification.

column8: Identify the task from the client's words
column15:
As an agent, I want autopilot to recognize which account task the client needs from their own phrasing, so that the right expert starts collecting without me categorizing the request.

=== TECHNICAL DESIGN ===
Keyword match first; LLM fallback. Once identified, build the expert prompt via buildTaskSystemPrompt(
profile, taskId) and run the first turn.
column16:
Scenario: Keyword match first, LLM fallback
  Given a client describes their need
  When keyword matching finds a task
  Then that task starts immediately
  And when keywords miss, an LLM classifies the intent before starting

column15: Score TASKS by keyword overlap, filtered by eligibleAccountTypes; zero LLM.
Classify intent when keywords miss; then build the expert prompt for turn 1.

column8: Route advice requests away from task collection
column15:
As a compliance owner, I want requests for financial advice to short-circuit to a callback before any task is identified, so that we never let an advice request masquerade as a transactional task.

=== TECHNICAL DESIGN ===
Test ADVICE_RE (and/or an LLM check) BEFORE matchTaskByIntent; if it is advice, set scope=callback so a
purchase task never swallows it.
column16:
Scenario: Advice short-circuit
  Given the client asks what they should buy
  When intent is evaluated
  Then it is routed to the callback scope, not the purchase task

column15:
Phase 2: a dedicated expert prompt for the identified task drives a reactive conversation. The expert knows which fields remain, the client's current account state, the field validation rules, and how to format the proposedAction JSON. It runs only when the client sends a new message (no polling) and exits with shouldExitAutopilot + a populated proposedAction when every field is confirmed.

Value: structured, reliable collection turns messy natural language into a clean, reviewable action.

=== TECHNICAL SCOPE ===
A per-task expert prompt (buildTaskSystemPrompt switch case) handles each subsequent message. It knows the
remaining fields (filterFields applies requiresMultipleAccounts / requiresAccountTypes / skipWhenFieldIs),
the current account state (summarizeAccounts header), the business rules, and the proposedAction JSON
contract. Exits with shouldExitAutopilot=true + proposedAction when all required fields are confirmed.
proposedAction shape: {taskId, fields:[{key,label,value}], ...} stamped via withSubmissionType.
column16:
Definition of done:
- The expert collects only the fields still needed, honoring conditional skips.
- It runs reactively (one turn per client message) and never double-sends.
- On completion it emits a well-formed proposedAction and exits.

column15:
As an agent, I want autopilot to gather every required field for the identified task by conversing with the client, so that a complete, structured request is assembled for me to review.

=== TECHNICAL DESIGN ===
Expert enumerates remaining fields and restates captured values; on completion emits the proposedAction
JSON; the server validates that all required (post-filter) fields are present before honoring the exit.
column16:
Scenario: Reactive collection to completion
  Given a task is in progress
  When the client answers a question
  Then the expert records it, asks only for what remains, and on the final field emits a proposedAction and exits

column15: Apply requiresMultipleAccounts / requiresAccountTypes / skipWhenFieldIs to compute remaining fields.
Emit {taskId, fields:[{key,label,value}]}; server validates all required fields before honoring exit.

column8: Follow up on partially answered prompts

As an investor, I want the assistant to notice when I answered only part of a multi-part question and ask for the rest, so that nothing required is silently skipped.

=== TECHNICAL DESIGN ===
Prompt rule (shared block): if multiple items were asked and only some answered, follow up for the rest
before moving on.
column16:
Scenario: Partial answer triggers follow-up
  Given the assistant asked for two pieces of information
  When I provide only one
  Then it follows up for the missing piece before moving on

column15:
Shared constants injected into all 19 task prompts so behavior is uniform: FORBIDDEN_TOPICS (financial advice, trade execution, fraud/security incidents, inheritance) each with a scripted response; a no-trades variant for tasks that legitimately handle trades; language rules for restating client-stated vs database-sourced facts; a no-repeat / frustrated-yes rule; and hallucination protection. Changing one constant changes every expert at once.

Value: consistent, compliant handling of sensitive topics regardless of which task is active.

=== TECHNICAL SCOPE ===
Shared constants injected into every task prompt: FORBIDDEN_TOPICS (advice, trades, fraud, inheritance -
each with a scripted response, plus the field-follow-up + restating-language rules) and
FORBIDDEN_TOPICS_NO_TRADES (same minus the trade clause, for trade-handling tasks). Editing one constant
changes all 19 experts - scope changes carefully. Plus a hallucination rule: never invent account facts.
column16:
Definition of done:
- All 19 experts share identical forbidden-topic handling.
- Restating language distinguishes client-stated from database-sourced facts.
- Experts do not invent account facts they were not given.

column15:
As a compliance owner, I want every task expert to handle advice, trades, fraud, and inheritance with the same scripted responses, so that sensitive topics are handled consistently no matter which task is active.

=== TECHNICAL DESIGN ===
Define FORBIDDEN_TOPICS once and inject it into every buildTaskSystemPrompt case; trade-handling tasks use
the FORBIDDEN_TOPICS_NO_TRADES variant.
column16:
Scenario: Uniform forbidden-topic response

  When the client raises a forbidden topic
  Then the expert gives the shared scripted response and routes appropriately

As an investor, I want the assistant to say "Got it - X will be Y" for what I just told it and "I see X is currently" for what it read from my account, so that I can trust which facts came from me versus from the system.

=== TECHNICAL DESIGN ===
Prompt rule: "Got it - X will be Y" for client-stated values; "I see X is currently" for database-sourced
values.

Scenario: Attribution language
  Given the assistant restates information
  Then client-stated values use "Got it" phrasing and database values use "I see ... currently" phrasing

column8: Prevent the assistant from inventing account facts
column15:
As a compliance owner, I want experts to refuse to fabricate account data they were not provided, so that clients are never given hallucinated balances, holdings, or statuses.

=== TECHNICAL DESIGN ===
Hallucination-protection rule in all system prompts; route unknown facts through client-tools (get_*) or
decline rather than guessing.
column16:
Scenario: No hallucinated data
  Given the expert lacks a specific account fact
  When the client asks for it
  Then it does not invent a value and instead fetches it or declines

column15:
The catalog of nineteen task experts. Each is a narrow, testable conversational specialist with its own field schema (including conditional skips and account-type/eligibility filters), business rules, and an execution type of real (writes data) or mock (returns a realistic confirmation). Each story below is one expert; the engine selects exactly one per conversation.

Value: per-task experts keep each flow precise and verifiable instead of blurring rules in one giant general agent.

=== TECHNICAL SCOPE ===
Each expert = a case in buildTaskSystemPrompt + a TASKS entry {id,name,keywords,fields[],executionType
real|mock, submissionType?} + an execute-task handler. Fields support requiresMultipleAccounts (skip the
account picker when the client has one account), requiresAccountTypes, skipWhenFieldIs (conditional skip),
and enum options. Adding a task = tasks.ts entry + prompt case + execute-task case.
column16:
Definition of done:
- Each expert collects exactly its required fields and honors its skips/eligibility.
- Each exits with a correctly shaped proposedAction stamped with its submission type.
- Real-execution experts write the right records; mock experts return clean confirmations.

As an IRA account holder, I want to add, update, or remove a beneficiary on an eligible account through chat, so that I control inheritance without paperwork, with the allocations validated.

=== TECHNICAL DESIGN ===
Fields: accountId (only if multiple accounts), action (Add/Update/Remove), beneficiaryName, relationship
(skip if Remove), percentage (skip if Remove), beneficiaryType Primary/Secondary (skip if Remove).
executionType REAL. Pre-prompt: fetch current beneficiaries from DynamoDB; filter accounts to IRA/SEP only
(iraAccounts) for BOTH the picker and the summarizeAccounts header. Enforce 100% allocation; acknowledge
existing beneficiaries; handle ADD/REMOVE/UPDATE/REPLACE-ALL. On execute, atomically replace that account's
beneficiaries, preserving others.
column16:
Scenario: Collect a beneficiary change
  Given I choose an eligible (IRA/SEP) account and an action
  When the action is add or update
  Then autopilot collects name, relationship, percentage, and primary/secondary
  And for remove it collects only the name

Scenario: Allocation and existing-beneficiary rules
  Given existing beneficiaries are present
  Then the expert acknowledges them and enforces a 100% total
  And taxable accounts are never offered for beneficiaries

Scenario: Real write on approval
  Given the Proposed Action is approved
  Then the beneficiaries for that account are atomically replaced while other accounts are preserved

column8: Fetch current beneficiaries before building the prompt
column15: Read existing beneficiaries from DynamoDB so the expert can acknowledge them.
Filter both the account picker and the summarizeAccounts header to IRA/SEP; never taxable.
Validate totals; support all four actions; atomic per-account replace on execute.

column15:
The conversational callback scope: it arranges a phone callback while the LLM never computes timestamps - it emits a day reference, hour, and minute, and the server resolves and validates the Eastern-Time to UTC instant (TZ-safe) and injects authoritative agent availability. Financial-advice requests are funneled here.

Value: reliable, correct scheduling (no timezone bugs) and a compliant home for advice requests.

=== TECHNICAL SCOPE ===
CALLBACK_PROMPT. The LLM NEVER computes timestamps: it emits {dayReference, hour24, minute}; the server
resolveCallbackTime (TZ-safe via Intl / date-fns-tz) resolves + validates the ET->UTC instant. The server
injects authoritative availability (describeCallbackAvailability) and composes the first-turn "why a
callback" opener deterministically. Advice requests short-circuit here (ADVICE_RE).
column16:
Definition of done:
- The model emits a structured time; the server resolves/validates ET->UTC.
- Real agent availability is injected into the conversation.
- Advice requests are handled in this scope.

and rejects invalid times; on success create the schedule via schedule-callback.

Scenario: Server resolves the time
  Given I describe a callback time conversationally
  When autopilot captures a day reference, hour, and minute
  Then the server resolves it to a validated ET->UTC instant and reflects real availability

describeCallbackAvailability into the prompt; server composes the first-turn why-a-callback line.

A post-hoc utility scope (locate-evidence) that, given a transcript and a Proposed Action, picks the authoritative transcript span for each collected field - the client's own statement, else the client-confirmed agent recap, never post-confirmation echoes. Every quote is validated server-side against the actual message text. Drives the evidence highlighting in the agent UI.

Value: lets agents verify, at a glance, exactly where each value in a Proposed Action came from.

=== TECHNICAL SCOPE ===
locate-evidence scope (LOCATE_EVIDENCE_PROMPT, model gpt-4o). Input {transcript, proposedAction}. One LLM
call picks the authoritative span per field (the client's statement, else the client-confirmed agent recap
- NEVER post-confirmation echoes). The SERVER validates every quote against the actual message text and
returns {evidence:[{fieldKey, messageId, start, end}]}. Early-returns before chat-turn logic; ANY failure
-> {evidence:[]} so the UI simply shows no highlights.
column16:
Definition of done:
- For each field it returns a validated {messageId, start, end} span or nothing.
- Post-confirmation echoes are never selected.
- Any failure yields an empty evidence set (no false highlights).

column8: Locate the transcript evidence for each collected field
column15:
As an agent, I want the system to identify where in the transcript each Proposed Action value was established, so that I can trust the card by seeing its source, and scrutinize anything unlocatable.

=== TECHNICAL DESIGN ===
Additive scope; for each field, validate that message.text.slice(start,end) equals the quoted span (reject
otherwise); return {evidence:[]} on any mismatch or failure.
column16:
Scenario: Validated spans only
  Given a Proposed Action and its transcript
  When evidence location runs
  Then each field maps to a validated client statement or confirmed recap span
  And degenerate or unverifiable cases return no evidence rather than a wrong span

column15:
The idle-check scope: when an agent has asked a question and the client goes quiet, an automatic timer triggers a gentle check-in message so the conversation does not stall.

Value: keeps conversations moving and reduces silent abandonment.

=== TECHNICAL SCOPE ===
idle-check scope. A client-side ~3-minute timer (started when the agent asks a question) triggers ONE
check-in turn. Guard against double-fire in React StrictMode (dev) and against firing while the client is
typing.
column16:
Definition of done:
- A check-in fires after the configured idle period following an agent question.
- It does not fire repeatedly or when the client is actively typing.

column8: Nudge a quiet client with a check-in
column15:
As an agent, I want autopilot to send a friendly check-in when the client goes quiet after I asked something, so that conversations do not stall and I do not have to babysit silence.

=== TECHNICAL DESIGN ===
Timer lives in the agent column; on expiry POST /autopilot-turn scope=idle-check exactly once; clear the
timer on any new message.
column16:
Scenario: Idle check-in
  Given I asked the client a question
  When the client is idle past the threshold
  Then autopilot sends a single check-in message

column15:
A four-column dashboard that lets a single representative handle up to four simultaneous client chats. Each column carries the client profile, the live chat thread, an AI support panel (suggested replies, predicted topics/questions, recommended articles, autopilot controls), and a Proposed Action card the agent reviews and approves. The workspace also manages incoming contacts, detects client disconnects, and auto-generates after-call work. The guiding principle: the AI does the work, the agent does the judgment.

Value: multiplies agent capacity and quality - more concurrent conversations, less typing and lookup, and consistent wrap-up.

=== ARCHITECTURE & STACK ===
The agent SPA (React 18 + Vite + TS), served at /agent. Telephony/chat via Amazon Connect Streams + an
embedded CCP. agentStore (Zustand) holds up to 4 ContactSlots {contactId, clientId, status, messages,
proposedAction, proposedActionEvidence, customerTyping, customerDisconnected, ...}. A useConnectStreams
hook wires CCP events (contact.onConnecting/onConnected/onEnded, chatSession.onMessage/onTyping) into the
store. Layout: AgentDesktop (4-column grid) + FocusingDesktop (single contact). Per column: ChatColumn,
ProposedActionCard, AISupport, IncomingAlert, IntentLabel, ResponseTimer, AfterCallWork, AutopilotMenu.
Connection tokens via the agent-connection Lambda; agent->customer messages/typing via send-agent-message.
GOTCHA: chatjs routes unmapped event types (e.g. participant.left) to onMessage - use that to detect the
customer leaving. Because Connect ends the whole contact on customer disconnect, hold the slot at 'active'
(don't auto-advance to ACW) until the agent clicks End chat.
column16:
Definition of done:
- An agent can run up to four concurrent chats with per-column context and AI support.
- Incoming contacts, disconnects, and ACW are handled in every UI mode.
- Proposed Actions can be reviewed, edited, and submitted with evidence visible.

column15:
The core desktop: an Amazon Connect CCP-backed, four-column layout where each column is one conversation slot showing client info, the chat thread, and AI support. Connection tokens are managed via the agent-connection Lambda; chat rendering and control messages are handled per column.

Value: the canvas that makes concurrent, context-rich handling possible.

=== TECHNICAL SCOPE ===
useConnectStreams subscribes to CCP/Streams events and maps them onto agentStore ContactSlots (max 4). The
agent-connection Lambda mints/refreshes the connection token (a raw fetch with the connection token is what
fixed the 403s on agent->customer sends). ChatColumn renders each slot's thread; control messages (agent
name, approval form, typing) are intercepted, not rendered.
column16:
Definition of done:
- Up to four slots render independently with correct client context.
- CCP connection and tokens are established reliably.
- Control messages (names, typing, approvals) are intercepted, not rendered as chat.

column8: Handle up to four chats at once
column15:
As an agent, I want a four-column desktop where each column is a separate client conversation, so that I can help several clients at once without losing track of any.

=== TECHNICAL DESIGN ===
agentStore keeps an array of <=4 slots keyed by contactId; each column reads only its slot so state never
bleeds across conversations.
column16:
Scenario: Independent slots
  Given I am handling multiple chats
  Then each column shows its own client info, thread, and AI support independently

As an agent, I want each column to show the client name, intent label, and account summary, so that I have the context I need without switching tools.

Scenario: Client context panel

column15:
New contacts arrive as an alert card with a countdown timer, the client name, the AI-generated intent summary, and the account types held; agents accept or skip. A bonus-opportunity badge surfaces when a contact qualifies for an incentive.

Value: agents triage incoming work quickly and with context, improving acceptance and prioritization.

=== TECHNICAL SCOPE ===
IncomingAlert renders on contact.onConnecting with the client name, AI intentSummary, account types, and a
ResponseTimer countdown. Accept routes the contact into a slot; skip releases it. Show a bonus badge when
the contact qualifies (metadata). Guard the Accept/Close handlers against double-fire in React StrictMode
(dev).
column16:
Definition of done:
- Incoming alerts show name, intent summary, accounts, and a countdown.
- Accept routes the contact into a slot; skip releases it.
- Bonus badges render when applicable.

column8: Triage incoming contacts with context and a timer
column15:
As an agent, I want incoming contacts to appear as a timed alert with the client name, intent summary, and accounts, so that I can decide to accept or skip with enough context and urgency.

Turns an approved Proposed Action into a real outcome. The execute-task Lambda performs the action - writing to DynamoDB for the tasks that own real data (beneficiaries, auto-invest, RMD settings) and returning realistic confirmations for the rest - always producing a traceable reference number and a structured confirmation the client sees as a green card. It also implements the submission-type framework, including the client-submitted (Type 3) approval round-trip.

Value: closes the loop - requests collected by AI and approved by an agent actually take effect, with an auditable confirmation.

=== ARCHITECTURE & STACK ===
execute-task Lambda (Node 20) behind POST /execute-task; dispatch by taskId. Real writes use the
lib-dynamodb DocumentClient (UpdateCommand) on the Clients table; mock tasks return a well-formed
confirmation. Every result carries referenceNumber = REF-XXXXXX. The confirmation is delivered to the
client chat (via send-agent-message) as a structured payload the customer renders as a green confirmation
card. submissionType is stamped on the proposedAction (withSubmissionType) at both return sites in
autopilot-turn.

- Type 3 actions execute only after the client approves, with identical confirmation.

switch(taskId){...}; mint REF-XXXXXX; return {referenceNumber, confirmation, ...}. Mock handlers return
canned confirmations; real handlers perform the DynamoDB write. Make real writes safe to retry.

For actions only the account owner may authorize (the first is adding an authorized user), the agent collects the details but cannot submit: their card button reads "Send to client", which places an editable "Your approval is required" card in the client's chat while the agent sees a waiting note. On the client's Submit, the agent app runs the exact same submit path as Type 1, so the confirmation is identical. Decline returns the card to the agent; agent cancel / chat-end / client-left all clean up the pending state, with an idempotency guard on the relay.

=== TECHNICAL SCOPE ===

declined). Agent sends the form -> customer renders ApprovalFormCard (editable fields, NO evidence buttons)
-> Submit sends client-approved -> the agent app runs the SAME submitProposedAction path as Type 1 (byte-
identical confirmation). Decline returns the card to the agent. Idempotency: flip awaitingClientApproval=
false BEFORE the await on the relay. Agent Cancel / chat-end / client-left all clean up the pending state.

The knowledge and prediction layer that makes both the client chat and the agent workspace feel anticipatory: a curated knowledge base (62 topics, 247 Q&A pairs) mapped to pages and wizard steps, plus three predictive services - predict-intent (relevant topics for the page/conversation), predict-questions (the client's likely next questions), and next-best-response (a suggested agent reply with an autopilot scope hint).

Value: reduces effort on both sides of the conversation and surfaces the right knowledge at the right moment.

=== ARCHITECTURE & STACK ===
kb.ts is a pure (React-free) data module: KB topics + ~247 Q&A + EXTRA_PAGE_TOPICS (page/step -> topic ids)
+ matchResources (article recommender). Three Nova Micro Lambdas behind their routes: predict-intent
(page+convo -> topics), predict-questions (convo -> 3 next questions), next-best-response (convo -> reply +
autopilot scope). Consumed by the customer chat pills (TopicButtons/QuestionButtons) and the agent AISupport
panel. Keep kb.ts importable by both frontends and lambdas.

topic ids; pageKeyFromPath derives the key from the route; a pageContextStore can publish a finer key. Keep
help-page slugs aligned with the link table.

For clients who prefer a phone call to a chat, the platform schedules a validated callback: the schedule-callback Lambda creates an Amazon EventBridge Scheduler event for the requested time, the execute-callback Lambda fires at that time (in production it would trigger an outbound call), and the cancel/reschedule task path lets clients change it. The agent app shows a callback confirmation with the scheduled time and a reference.

Value: meets clients on their preferred channel and guarantees the call is scheduled at the right instant.

=== ARCHITECTURE & STACK ===
schedule-callback Lambda creates a one-time Amazon EventBridge Scheduler schedule targeting the
execute-callback Lambda at the resolved UTC instant. The time is computed + validated SERVER-side (never by
the LLM - see the autopilot callback scope, which emits {dayReference,hour24,minute} and lets the server
resolve ET->UTC). The agent app shows a callback confirmation (time + REF). cancel-reschedule-callback (a
standard task) updates or deletes the schedule.

Definition of done:
- A requested callback creates a scheduled event at the correct (validated) time.
- The event fires at the scheduled time and is observable.
- Clients can cancel or reschedule via the standard task flow.

Value: dependable scheduling with no timezone or availability surprises.

=== TECHNICAL SCOPE ===
schedule-callback creates the EventBridge schedule (no flexible window); persist {callbackId, scheduledUtc,
clientId, REF}. CallbackScheduler renders in chat; reflect availability from describeCallbackAvailability.

column15:
A single source of truth for fund reference data and a separate live-price service. The fund catalog (the full lineup) is seeded into a DynamoDB table from one canonical definition and served via an API with caching; every content page, picker, and all three AI systems read the same catalog, so they can never disagree about what funds exist or what they cost. Live intraday prices and returns come from a dedicated market-data service whose symbol map is derived from the catalog (so it cannot drift).

Value: consistency and trust - one update propagates everywhere, and pages never contradict the assistant.

=== ARCHITECTURE & STACK ===
One canonical fund data file (pure data, framework-free) is the single edit point, the SEED for the Funds
DynamoDB table (PK ticker), and the frontend offline fallback. At runtime the TABLE is the source of truth:
the get-funds Lambda serves GET /funds (module-cached ~60min); the useFunds() hook consumes it (localStorage
TTL + bundled fallback). The backend reaches the canonical file through ONE bridge module (re-exports the
fund list + a FUND_PICKLIST). AI access: a get_funds tool (reads the table) for bot/NBR/experts, plus
FUND_PICKLIST injected into expert prompts. Live prices/returns come from a separate market-data Lambda
whose ticker->symbol map is DERIVED from the catalog (so it cannot drift). Seed/refresh a fresh env via
GET /reset-funds?key=...
column16:
Definition of done:
- The catalog is the runtime source of truth, served with caching and an offline fallback.
- Content pages, pickers, and all AI systems read the same catalog.
- Live prices come from market data whose symbols derive from the catalog.

column15:
One canonical fund definition seeds a DynamoDB catalog table; an API serves it (module-cached) and a client hook consumes it with a local cache and a bundled offline fallback. A per-environment seed/refresh endpoint repopulates it.

Value: a single edit point that fans out to every consumer, eliminating drift.

=== TECHNICAL SCOPE ===
Canonical file -> seed table (one item per fund). get-funds Scans once and module-caches; useFunds reads
/funds with a localStorage TTL + a bundled fallback. ~36 small items that change < once/day, so a single
Scan + cache is correct. reset-funds (key-gated) seeds/refreshes per environment.

Definition of done:

- The serving API is cached; the client hook caches and falls back offline.
- A seed/refresh endpoint repopulates a fresh environment.

Scans + caches. useFunds reads /funds with localStorage TTL and falls back to the bundled catalog offline.

  Then it reads the same catalog (cached) with a bundled offline fallback
